text.title: GitLab Boards — Agentic Workflow and Automation
text.summary: How to drive GitLab boards through CI and subagents, including label-curator and triage-router patterns and their failure modes.

section.title: Agentic Architecture

rule.statement: Encode the workflow as scoped labels in a YAML file (.gitlab/labels.yaml) applied via a CI job — never configure labels manually at scale.
rule.rationale: Manual label configuration drifts across projects in a group; idempotent CI application (via gitlabform) keeps all projects consistent.

section.p: CI moves issues across columns on MR events: open → review, merge → closed. Never let agents close issues without an MR or human approval — closure is a decision-authority boundary.
section.reference: Audit script for issues idle in workflow::in-progress beyond N days

list.item: label-curator-agent (haiku): nightly walk over issues; flags issues with multiple workflow:: labels, missing type::, stale workflow::in-progress older than 14 days.
triage-router-agent (sonnet): incoming issues from Service Desk and external bots — applies type:: + workflow::triage, suggests assignee from CODEOWNERS.
iteration-planner-agent (sonnet): converts sprint planning template into an actual planning issue with capacity table from team availability.
mr-board-mover-agent (haiku): runs in CI on MR events; flips workflow::in-progress to workflow::review based on MR state. Idempotent.
dependency-graph-agent (sonnet): walks /blocks and /relate links, produces Mermaid dependency graph for sprint planning.
password-scrubber-agent: runs over issue descriptions, comments, and Service Desk emails before any external sharing.

example.note: Two-step: ingest issue → emit structured label assignment:

antipattern.description: Agent applies two workflow:: labels; GitLab keeps the last one silently.
antipattern.reason: Scoped-label mutual exclusivity is enforced server-side by keeping the last label applied; the agent believes both stuck. Always verify with a read-after-write API call.

antipattern.description: Agent generates an MR body with "Closes #999" without verifying the issue exists.
antipattern.reason: Auto-close hallucination: the referenced issue may not exist or may be in a different project; validate IDs before committing.

antipattern.description: Agent uses REST PUT /issues to trigger Quick Actions like /spend or /assign.
antipattern.reason: Quick Actions only execute when submitted through the notes API or the issue body on creation/update — not via field updates in REST PUT.

antipattern.description: Notification spam: bots comment on every state change.
antipattern.reason: Thread updates drown teams; prefer updating the existing thread rather than posting new comments per transition.

list.item: Mandatory human checkpoints: label taxonomy changes, milestone closure, iteration cadence changes, board deletion, mass relabel, Service Desk priority::critical auto-assignment.
Service Desk emails are PII; scrub before any external model call.
Long-context drift: agents reading 1,000 issues at once lose track — page by board column or by label.
